commands.review_summary:
This branch assigns Blacksmith runner defaults to canonical CI Node/check shards, updates the generated shard runner plan and tests, and revises CI runner docs.

PR surface: Tests +17, Docs 0, Config 0, Other +2. Total +19 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows missing matrix.runner values fall back to ubuntu-24.04 and the shard generator omits runner for several shards; the PR body also reports a live sample where 270/270 affected jobs ran on GitHub-hosted runners.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer CI/ops acceptance or live exact-head CI observation, not an automated code repair.

target.title: ci: consolidate Blacksmith runner defaults
target.body:
## Summary
- assign a Blacksmith default runner to every normal core Node CI shard so missing matrix runner fields no longer fall back to GitHub-hosted Ubuntu on canonical PR/push runs
- keep workflow dispatch and non-canonical repository fallback behavior on GitHub-hosted runners
- correct the CI docs to remove the stale `scripts/ci-runner-labels.mjs` queue-aware fallback description and document current static runner routing

## Verification
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-node-test-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- dry shard probe: 36 normal Node shards, 0 missing runner labels, 0 GitHub-hosted runner labels
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings

## Live CI runner evidence
- Affected surface: 27 normal non-dist core Node shards that previously omitted an explicit runner and could therefore use the workflow fallback.
- Current live behavior sample: 10 recent completed CI runs inspected with the GitHub Actions jobs API showed 270/270 affected jobs running with labels `["ubuntu-24.04"]` and runner group `GitHub Actions`; 0/270 used Blacksmith.
- Minute exposure sample: those 270 affected jobs totaled 385.2 GitHub-hosted job-minutes, or about 38.5 affected GitHub-hosted minutes per CI run in the sample.
- CI volume context: `gh run list` reached the 1000-run page cap across roughly 17 hours of CI history, so this fallback pattern is on a very high-volume path.
- Spot-check example: run `26555781983`, job `checks-node-core-fast`, reported labels `["ubuntu-24.04"]`, runner group `GitHub Actions`, runner name `GitHub Actions 1003834230`.
- Post-patch source proof: the dry shard probe reports 36 normal Node shards, 0 missing runner labels, and 0 GitHub-hosted runner labels; 27 affected non-dist core Node shards now emit `blacksmith-8vcpu-ubuntu-2404`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Normal canonical Linux CI matrix shards no longer use GitHub-hosted Ubuntu as the implicit fallback when a Node shard omits `matrix.runner`.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree with dependencies installed; workflow syntax checked with repo workflow sanity tooling; live GitHub Actions job metadata sampled from recent completed CI runs before this PR head is merged.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-node-test-plan.test.ts`; `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`; `pnpm docs:list`; `git diff --check`; Node dry probe of `createNodeTestShards({ includeReleaseOnlyPluginShards: false })`; GitHub Actions jobs API sample of 10 recent completed CI runs for the 27 affected job names.
Evidence after fix: The dry probe reported 36 normal Node shards with 0 missing runner labels and 0 `ubuntu-24.04` runner labels; the targeted Vitest file passed 13 tests; the live pre-patch sample confirmed the affected jobs are currently landing on GitHub-hosted runners.
Observed result after fix: Core Node shard planning now emits explicit Blacksmith runner labels, and CI workflow fallback expressions default canonical runs to Blacksmith labels while keeping manual/fork GitHub-hosted fallback behavior.
What was not tested: No fresh PR-head GitHub Actions run has yet been observed routing these affected jobs to Blacksmith after the patch; the current proof combines live pre-patch runner metadata with local workflow/shard verification of the new labels.
